verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a cytoplasmic heat shock protein (Hsp70 family) with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or other membrane-anchoring mechanism. It functions in cytosolic and organellar protein folding and the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway. The NCBI summary and gene-group membership (Heat shock 70kDa proteins, TIM23 complex) confirm a strictly intracellular localization. No documented surface isoform, lysosomal-exocytosis route, dual-localization pool, or covalent/stable tethering to a surface partner has been established. Not a target of surface-directed therapeutics. pMHC presentation of derived peptides does not establish surface accessibility of the protein body.
